title: Synthesis of some Hydroxy Cyclohexyl Carbinol Phosphates and Related Compounds
author: S. K. Chakraborti
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
In order to study which atoms or functional groups of glucoso-6-phosphate are responsible for binding to the enzyme glucose-6-phospha.te dehydrogenase, a series of hydroxylated cyclohexane carbinol phosphates and related compounds which resembled glucose-6-phosphate sterically, were synthesised. The aromatic carboxylic acids or esters containing hydroxy groups in appropriate position were reduced by catalytic hydrogenation into the  cis - and  trans -cyclohexane derivatives which were then converted to 
the corresponding hydroxy cyclohexane carbinols by reduction with lithium aluminium hydride. The carbinols were phosphorylated to give the diphenyl-phosphoryl esters which on hydrogenolysis gave the desired phosphates (IV-IX). Synthesis of 2-hydroxymethyl tetrahydrofuran, 2-hydroxymethyl tetra hydropyran, and 2-hydroxy-6-hydroxymethyl-tetrahydropyran phosphates (X, XI, XVI) have also been accomplished. Evaluation of these compounds, however, revealed that they were weakly inhibitory or non-inhibitory to the enzyme glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase.
steps_raw:

The aromatic carboxylic acids or esters containing hydroxy groups in appropriate position were reduced by catalytic hydrogenation into the  cis - and  trans -cyclohexane derivatives which were then converted to 
the corresponding hydroxy cyclohexane carbinols by reduction with lithium aluminium hydride. Synthesis of 2-hydroxymethyl tetrahydrofuran, 2-hydroxymethyl tetra hydropyran, and 2-hydroxy-6-hydroxymethyl-tetrahydropyran phosphates (X, XI, XVI) have also been accomplished.
